mechanism: Selective BCL-2 inhibition, promotes apoptosis
additional_notes: Very potent in BCL2-dependent AML; clinical use in AML

mechanism: FLT3 (FLT3-ITD) tyrosine kinase inhibition
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; highly potent against FLT3-ITD

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL tyrosine kinases
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3-mutant relapsed AML

mechanism: Multi-kinase inhibition including FLT3
additional_notes: Approved in combination therapy for FLT3-mutant AML; less potent than quizartinib/gilteritinib

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including RAF, VEGFR, and FLT3
additional_notes: Activity in some FLT3-ITD AMLs; reported ranges span low nM to low µM

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA synthesis
additional_notes: Also called Ara-C; standard AML chemotherapy backbone

mechanism: DNA methyltransferase inhibition leading to hypomethylation
additional_notes: Used clinically in older/unfit AML patients; exposure-dependent potency

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA/DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases
additional_notes: Similar clinical use and potency profile to decitabine

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Shows activity in some AML models; clinical combination studies exist

mechanism: Histone deacetylase inhibition leading to altered chromatin and gene expression
additional_notes: Potent pan-HDAC inhibitor with AML preclinical activity

mechanism: Histone deacetylase inhibition (broad HDAC profile)
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; activity variable by cell line

mechanism: Allosteric inhibition of MEK1/2, blocking MAPK pathway signaling
additional_notes: Effective where MAPK signaling drives proliferation or in combinations

mechanism: Inhibits CDK1/2/5/9, disrupting cell cycle and transcriptional CDK functions
additional_notes: Potent CDK inhibitor that induces apoptosis in AML models

mechanism: Selective CDK4/6 inhibition, causing G1 cell-cycle arrest
additional_notes: Activity in AML is cell-context dependent; many AML lines less dependent on CDK4/6

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases and ABL among others
additional_notes: Repurposed in subsets of AML and in combination studies; activity line-dependent

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with oxidative-stress–mediated cytotoxicity in AML

mechanism: Multiple reported mechanisms (mitochondrial uncoupling, Wnt/STAT3 inhibition reported)
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate shown to inhibit AML proliferation in vitro

mechanism: Covalent inhibition of BTK, affecting BCR and related signaling pathways
additional_notes: Activity in AML is variable and often subset- or combination-dependent

mechanism: Inhibits protein synthesis (elongation) leading to decreased oncoprotein levels
additional_notes: Also known as homoharringtonine; approved for CML and shows AML activity

mechanism: Inhibit BET family bromodomains (e.g., BRD4), altering transcriptional programs
additional_notes: Examples include birabresib (OTX015) and JQ1 (JQ1 often ~0.1–1 µM in AML)
